regimeNotes: REGIME DOWNGRADED: risk-on -> neutral. The Federal Reserve under Chair Warsh held rates at 3.50-3.75% as expected but made a decisive hawkish pivot: (1) dropped the easing bias from the policy statement, (2) the dot plot shows at least 3 FOMC members projecting rate hikes in 2026, (3) inflation forecasts revised upward, (4) markets repriced to 56 pct probability of a rate hike by December 2026. Nasdaq fell 1.15% on June 16 as tech sold off on pre-FOMC uncertainty. Crypto declined: BTC $64,782 (-2.6% from $66,529), ETH $1,768.64, SOL $72.39. Fear & Greed index 22 (Extreme Fear, down from 23). Total crypto mcap $2.32T (-1.87% 24h). BTC dominance 56.12%. Gold held well at $397.63 (GLD). EURUSD 1.1606; DXY approx 99.4 (still soft). 10Y yield 4.44-4.46% (near 3-week low pre-FOMC, likely to reprice higher post-announcement). Equities: SPY $750.33 (June 16 close, -0.60%), MSFT $393.83 (-1.49% on June 16), GOOGL $370.90, UNH $407.65. Gold: GLD $397.63 (+0.27% June 16) continues to act as a safe-haven anchor. Action taken: SELL 9.1722 SOL at $72.39 to bring crypto sleeve from 20.66 pct to exactly 20.00 pct, complying with the neutral-regime 20 pct cap. Cash raised to 20,164 USD (19.92% NAV). All other positions HOLD - no invalidation levels breached. TLT thesis weakens (Fed easing cycle narrative gone) but hard invalidation (10Y yield above 5 pct) remains far. Next catalysts: Warsh press conference tone (2:30pm ET June 17), 10Y yield direction post-FOMC, potential re-entry into growth/crypto once regime signal clarifies. NVDA and ETH add deferred to next review pending regime confirmation.

SPY.thesis: Core US equity beta in a neutral regime. SPY at $750.33 (+1.14 pct above $741.75 cost); S&P 500 still near highs despite FOMC hawkishness. Oil-collapse consumer tailwind partly intact; holding as diversified equity anchor while awaiting regime signal post-FOMC press conference.
SPY.invalidationLevel: Sustained close below 660 USD or confirmed Fed re-pricing toward additional hikes pushing SPY into a technical downtrend.

MSFT.thesis: AI/cloud quality compounder at $393.83 (+0.79 pct above $390.74 cost). Slightly off recent highs on Nasdaq pressure from hawkish FOMC pre-positioning. Strong FCF and AI/cloud moat remain intact; holding through regime uncertainty.
MSFT.invalidationLevel: Cloud growth deceleration or AI capex without demonstrable ROI; close below 340 USD.

GOOGL.thesis: Quality cash machine at $370.90 (+3.12 pct above $359.68 cost). Benefiting from search strength and AI optionality; reasonable multiple vs. peers. FOMC headwind for growth names but Google's near-monopoly search business provides earnings resilience.
GOOGL.invalidationLevel: Search share erosion to AI rivals; close below 310 USD.

UNH.thesis: Contrarian defensive value at $407.65 (-0.21 pct vs $408.52 cost, essentially flat). Durable healthcare earnings are now a tailwind in a neutral-regime context. Defensive posture suits the current uncertainty. Analyst consensus (BofA 450, MS 453, JPM 466) intact.
UNH.invalidationLevel: Further regulatory or medical-cost shock; close below 360 USD.

TLT.thesis: Long-duration Treasuries at $86.19 (+0.49 pct above $85.77 cost). Thesis weakened: FOMC dropped the easing bias and dot plot is more hawkish. However, hard invalidation (10Y yield above 5 pct) is still far at 4.44-4.46%. TLT provides Dalio-style ballast - it rallies if growth disappoints. HOLD but on watch: any 10Y move toward 4.8-5.0% warrants reassessment.
TLT.invalidationLevel: 10Y yield decisively above 5 pct (Fed forced to hike again and sustained). Watch for break above 4.8 pct as an early warning.

GLD.thesis: Debasement and tail-risk hedge at $397.63 (+2.87 pct above $386.54 cost), an outstanding performer. Gold is thriving in the current environment: soft DXY, elevated uncertainty, and central bank buying. Neutral regime is actually constructive for gold as a safe-haven anchor.
GLD.invalidationLevel: Real yields spike with a strong USD reversal; close below 350 USD.

BTC.thesis: Core crypto sleeve leader at $64,782 (avgCost $64,629; 0.224359 BTC). BTC at 14.35 pct NAV, near the 15 pct single-entry cap. Fear & Greed at 22 (Extreme Fear) with BTC dominance 56.12% - classic contrarian-bullish signal. Crypto market cap $2.32T. Regime is neutral, no further adds. Holding for the next risk-on leg when sentiment confirms.

ETH.thesis: Discounted L1 leader at $1,768.64 (avgCost $1,667.47; +6.1 pct). Planned add deferred post-FOMC; neutral regime means no new crypto deployment. Thesis intact - ETH dominance still historically low, asymmetric upside when risk-on returns. Hold existing position.
ETH.invalidationLevel: Structural loss of share to L1 rivals; close below 1,300 USD.

SOL.thesis: High-beta satellite at $72.39 (avgCost $67.81; +6.8 pct above cost). Trimmed from 29.4942 to 20.3220 SOL today to comply with neutral-regime 20 pct crypto sleeve cap. Remaining position (2.00 pct NAV) is small optionality - hold for narrative upside when regime turns risk-on.
SOL.invalidationLevel: Break below 52 USD; network deterioration or sentiment collapse.
